calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Gee (Personal Representative, in pro per); Christopher Hayes (former counsel for Julie Vredenburg, appearing on his own behalf).

Mr. Hayes presented argument as to why his lien for attorney's fees should be included in the Order for Final Distribution for which Mr. Gee provided rebuttal. After brief discussion, the Court found that the lien was valid in the amount of $5,046.05 and granted Mr. Hayes' request to include payment of the same in the Order for Final Distribution as a charge against the distributive share of Julie Vredenburg. Mr. Gee is to prepare a revised Order for review by the Court Examiner.

Judicial Office: Stella Pantazis, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: David Davenport CSR #1694; Bailiff: Deputy Jimmy Peralta; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnson.

Petition To Remove Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-04-2006 Off Calendar

For Waiver Of Appraisal By Probate Referee
Off Calendar Nov-15-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Paul Gee appearing in pro per. Court received a letter from the objector stating that she is withdrawing her objections. Matter granted. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

For Waiver Of Appraisal By Probate Referee
Off Calendar Oct-25-2006 Continued To Nov-15-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Paul Gee appearing as petitioner in pro per. Christopher Hays appearing for daughter Julie P. Vredenburg. Mr. Hays has filed a petition to remove the administrator yesterday. Hearing is set on 12/4. Matter is continued to 11/15. Court advises Mr. Gee to retain counsel. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

For Waiver Of Appraisal By Probate Referee
Off Calendar Oct-18-2006 Continued To Oct-25-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Paul P. Gee appearing in pro per. Christopher Hays appearing for Julie Vredenburg, daughter. The objections filed do not have anything to do with the petition. Mr. Hays argues that estate properties are not listed on the inventory. He wants the Probate Referee to look for those items. Court informs Mr. Hays that it is not the Probate Referee's responsibility to be an investigator. Mr. Gee has filed a rebuttal on Monday but the Court and Mr. Hays have not seen it. Mr. Gee gave a copy to Mr. Hays in open court. Matter is continued to 10/25. Judge: Raymond J. Arata. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-05-2006 Granted Per Written Order
